title: Jenna Zan Arbor
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Citation}} {{Character |type=Galactic Empire |image=[[File:Jenna_Zan_Arbor.jpg]] |name=Jenna Zan Arbor |homeworld=[[Ventrux]]''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Evil Experiment]]'' |birth=Before [[68 BBY/Legends|68 BBY]], Ventrux |death=[[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]''[[The Last of the Jedi: Reckoning]]'' |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]]''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Deadly Hunter]]'' |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blonde]]Even though an image of Zan Arbor from ''[[Ghosts of the Sith]]'' shows her with red hair, she is stated to have blonde hair numerous times during the [[Star Wars: Jedi Apprentice|''Jedi Apprentice'']] and [[Star Wars: Jedi Quest|''Jedi Quest'']] series. |feathers= |eyes=Gray |skin=White |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Arbor Industries]] *[[Blackwater Systems]]''[[Jedi Quest: The False Peace]]'' *[[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]'' *[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]''[[The Last of the Jedi: Against the Empire]]'' |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|You are a subject of a scientific experimentation. You will not be hurt, only studied.|Jenna Zan Arbor to Qui-Gon Jinn|}} '''Jenna Zan Arbor''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], and a deranged [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Ventrux]]. Throughout her career, she had numerous confrontations with the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], she was a leading scientist working for the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. When the war had ended, she had been drafted into working for the new [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. ==Biography== ===Early career=== {{Quote|When famine struck Rend 5 and I bio-engineered a new food to feed the entire planet, did I get a reward? When the Tendor Virus struck the entire Caldoni system and my vaccine cured millions, what did I receive in return? Not enough. I learned my lesson.''"
"''What did you learn?''"
"''That I must not depend on the galaxy to recognize my greatness. I must depend on myself to raise the funds I need.|Zan Arbor explains her past conflicts to Qui-Gon Jinn|}} At the beginning of her career, Jenna Zan Arbor was a famed [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] and the head of [[Arbor Industries]]. She was known for saving a planet from a pandemic after creating a vaccine for a deadly virus. Zan Arbor was also known to help planets with little technology such as [[Melasaton]] overcome their burdens. However, nobody knew that she actually started the problems in the first place, to gain credits and improve her reputation. ====Force experiments==== {{Quote|I am monitoring your bodily functions. When you access the Force, your body temperature drops. Your heartbeats slows. So strange. Once I thought the Force would have the opposite effect. But it works mysteriously. That is why it is so interesting to study.|Zan Arbor studies the captive Qui-Gon|}} Zan Arbor became interested in how [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] worked and began conducting experiments. She had connections to the [[Senator/Legends|senator]] of [[Belasco/Legends|Belasco]], [[Uta S'orn]] who kept her dark deeds quiet in the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. Unbeknownst to Senator S'orn, her son [[Ren S'orn|Ren]], who was [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] (although his mother had taken him away from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] when she was unable to cope with losing him) was being studied by Zan Arbor. These experiments lead to Ren's death, something Zan Arbor didn't expect. Feeling somewhat remorseful, she felt she needed a subject stronger in the Force, so she hired a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], [[Ona Nobis]], to capture the "retired" [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Noor R'aya]] and bring him to her [[Jenna Zan Arbor's secret laboratory|research facility]] on [[Simpla-12]].''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Dangerous Rescue]]'' Then, weeks later, another [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] named [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] was knocked out by Nobis in the [[Cascardi Mountains]] on [[Duneeden]]. He was taken to the laboratory, where Zan Arbor tortured him to the point of near death. However, Qui-Gon was rescued by [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], along with [[Astri Oddo]], who was disguised as Ona Nobis. Yet Zan Arbor warned the Jedi that she had another in custody—Noor R'aya—and threatened to inject him with poison should Qui-Gon leave the building. She killed her assistant, [[Nil]], as he was a liability. Zan Arbor then made plans to leave the planet, contacting [[Cholly]], [[Weez]] and [[Tup (thief)|Tup]], who were droid dealers to arrange shipment of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]]. But the trio were helping Obi-Wan, [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]] and [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]] get into her lab. ====Belasco==== {{Quote|After all your experiments with the Force, in the end you failed to understand its power.''"
"''I should have killed you when I could."
"That was your mistake.|Zan Arbor is captured by Qui-Gon Jinn|}} With the droids all destroyed, Zan Arbor escaped the planet with Noor. She fled to Belasco, where she engineered a virus from natural waterborne organisms on the planet. She planned to infect the populace through their water supply, designing the bacteria to proliferate if threatened. With S'orn's help, she was able to infiltrate the containment facilities to introduce the bacteria. The Jedi caught up to her, with the data to cure Belasco. As Qui-Gon trapped her under a tapestry, she deeply regretted that she didn't kill him when she had the chance. Both Zan Arbor and Uta S'orn were sent to a penal colony for life. The solution for the bacteria Zan Arbor introduced was found on her [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] and the water was fixed. ===Galactic criminal=== [[File:Arbor.jpg|thumb|Jenna Zan Arbor]] Around [[25 BBY/Legends|25 BBY]], Zan Arbor somehow escaped the penal colony. She settled on the planet [[Vanqor/Legends|Vanqor]] and began perfecting a drug known as the [[Zone of Self-Containment]]. With this drug, she was able to capture [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and many other innocent people; however, she was forced to flee Vanqor when Obi-Wan Kenobi, [[Siri Tachi/Legends|Siri Tachi]] and [[Ferus Olin]] came to rescue Anakin. Fleeing Vanqor, Zan Arbor teamed up with [[Granta Omega]] who agreed to fund her next project.''[[Jedi Quest: The Moment of Truth]]'' She went into hiding on [[Romin]], under the protection of [[Roy Teda]] and her [[Phlog/Legends|Phlog]] bodyguard [[Hue]]. When Teda was overthrown, the Jedi came to capture them, but they escaped yet again.''[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard]]'' In [[24 BBY/Legends|24 BBY]], Zan Arbor then constructed the [[Blackwater Systems]] facility on [[Falleen (planet)/Legends|Falleen]] to create machines that would be used to spread the Zone of Self-Containment. The Jedi once again foiled her plot and she was forced to destroy the facility. The Jedi chased her to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where she and Granta Omega orchestrated a [[Attack on the Senate Building|failed attempt]] to assassinate [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], and finally to [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], where she and Omega were [[Mission to Korriban (23 BBY)|cornered]] by the Jedi in the [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]]. When the Jedi killed Omega, Zan Arbor fled.''[[Jedi Quest: The Final Showdown]]'' She went into hiding and went unnoticed by both the Jedi and the Republic. That same year, when [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] publicly declared the formation of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Zan Arbor was hired to be one of their leading scientists.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' ===The Clone Wars=== Now a scientist for the Separatists, Zan Arbor created devastating agents of biological warfare. A few months before the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]], she worked with [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] genetic researchers to create the [[Stone mite/Legends|stone mites]], insects that could devour entire buildings in mere hours. These stone mites were unleashed on a number of worlds. Two months into the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Zan Arbor successfully perfected a deadly [[Swamp gas|gas poison]] that was used to wipe out the entire [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] colony on the [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] moon of [[Ohma-D'un/Legends|Ohma-D'un]].''[[Star Wars: Republic: The New Face of War]]'' A form of this same gas was incorporated into the defense mechanism of Viceroy [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray's]] [[Mechno-chair/Legends|mechno-chair]]. ===Early Empire=== {{Quote|Lord Vader. I didn't realize I had the honor of your personal reception. I would have emerged sooner.''"
"''Do not start our acquaintance with a lie.''"
"''All right. I made you wait to establish power. It's something I'm in the habit of doing. From now on, let us agree to be honest throughout our dealings. It is more efficient.''"
"''Precisely.|Zan Arbor meets Darth Vader|}} A year after [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] was carried out, Zan Arbor was spotted on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] by [[Ferus Olin]] and [[Trever Flume]] followed by a high-ranking Imperial Officer. She commanded the officer to bring her to the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]].{{InsiderCite|88|Ghosts of the Sith}} Later, on [[Bellassa/Legends|Bellassa]], Olin discovered evidence possibly linking her to a [[Death Star/Legends|secret Imperial project]]. Zan Arbor was working with scientists who had been forcibly taken from their homeworlds to help with this operation,''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]'' though weapons systems weren't in her repertoire. She had been working to create a [[Selective memory erasing drug|memory-erasing serum]] that would completely remove specific unpleasant memories. Scientist [[Linna Naltree]] was with her when the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] Darth Vader came to call for the serum, seeking to eliminate his memory of [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]]. In return for her services, she would have an [[Jenna Zan Arbor's apartment|apartment]] in [[500 Republica/Legends|500 Republica]], a personal visit to and starship designed for her by [[Raith Sienar/Legends|Raith Sienar]], and a high level security pass. After Vader left, Zan Arbor and her assistant ran into fugitives [[Trever Flume]] and [[Ry-Gaul]]. As the two attacked the scientist, Linna administered the serum via syringe to Zan Arbor, wiping her memory slowly. As she screamed over her fading formulas and fifty years of work, Trevor Flume destroyed her personal datapad, eliminating the threat of Jenna Zan Arbor once and for all. Now an empty shell of her former self, Zan Arbor was left with no identity and a vague sense that she had once been a brilliant genius. Later, when Vader visited Zan Arbor after she failed to respond to any of his messages, he found her trying to remember her favorite tea, and misremembering her homeworld to be the planet [[Moseum]] instead of Ventrux. As she was unable to help him forget Padmé, Vader killed her in a flash of anger. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Jenna Zan Arbor}} *''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Deadly Hunter]]'' {{1st}} *''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Evil Experiment]]'' *''[[Jedi Apprentice: The Dangerous Rescue]]'' *''[[Jedi Quest: The Moment of Truth]]'' *''[[Jedi Quest: The Changing of the Guard]]'' *''[[Jedi Quest: The False Peace]]'' *''[[Jedi Quest: The Final Showdown]]'' *''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]'' {{Mo}} *{{InsiderCite|88|Ghosts of the Sith}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Secret Weapon]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Against the Empire]]'' *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Master of Deception]]'' *''[[The Last of the Jedi: Reckoning]]'' ==Sources== *''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' *{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/81/indexp7.html|text=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena|int=Droids, Technology and the Force: A Clash of Phenomena (article)}} *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *{{DB|character|jennazanarbor|Arbor, Jenna Zan}} *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Jenna Zan Arbor |es=Jenna Zan Arbor |fi=Jenna Zan Arbor |deGA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Zan Arbor, Jenna}} [[Category:Biologists]] [[Category:Confederacy science and engineering personnel]] [[Category:Criminals]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Galactic Empire science and engineering personnel]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Torturers and interrogators]]
